info.name: ECommerce-Project API
info.description:
Full test/documentation suite for the ECommerce-Project API (products, categories, cart, orders, auth), served under /api/v1. Self-contained single-file collection - baseUrl/categoryId/productId/orderId are collection variables (Collection > Variables), no separate environment file needed. 

Two rate limiters apply: a generalLimiter (100 req/15min/IP) in front of every route, and an authLimiter (10 req/15min/IP) in front of the whole /api/v1/auth router - so it covers register as well as login, not just login. 

Run folders top-to-bottom (Auth -> Categories -> Products -> Cart -> Orders & Checkout -> Misc -> Cleanup) via Collection Runner; later requests depend on tokens/ids captured by earlier ones. Prerequisite: run `npm run seed` first so the seeded admin account (admin@example.com / AdminPass123) exists. Each request carries one saved response example (Examples tab) reflecting the current source code; these are accurate reconstructions rather than live captures (this sandbox has no route to a live MongoDB) - re-run against your dev server and use 'Save Response > Save as Example' for byte-exact captures if needed.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Register/login endpoints. POST /register always creates a customer role account, so the seeded admin (admin@example.com / AdminPass123, see seed.js) is the only way to get an admin token. /api/v1/auth (both /register AND /login) now shares one authLimiter bucket: 10 requests / 15 minutes per IP - this folder alone uses ~6 of those 10 per run (2 registers + 4 logins), so two consecutive full runs within 15 minutes can trip it on later requests.

item.name: Products
item.description: GET routes are public. POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE require an admin Bearer token. productId is a collection variable written by the first create request below. GET /api/v1/products is now paginated and sorted: response shape is data: { products, pagination: { total, page, limit, totalPages } } - not a bare array. Supports page, limit (capped at 50, default 10), sortBy (price/name/createdAt/stock, default createdAt), order (asc/desc, default desc), fields (whitelist projection), plus the existing category/minPrice/maxPrice/inStock/search filters. The list endpoint no longer populates category (raw id only) - GET /api/v1/products/:id still does.

request.description: inStock now filters on the stored `inStock` boolean field directly (reverted from filtering by live `stock` count). That field defaults to true and nothing in the current codebase updates it when stock changes via orders/cart, so it can drift from the real stock level over time - worth knowing if you rely on it.

item.name: Get Product By Id - Not Found (404)

response.name: Forbidden - customer role cannot update products

request.description: Heads up: the controller builds the update as a plain object {name, category, price, stock} with no $set wrapper. MongoDB treats an update object with no operators as a full-document replacement, so fields this endpoint doesn't list - images, description, inStock - are not carried over. This looks like a pre-existing gap from before the `images` field was added (PATCH is unaffected and safe for partial updates). Worth a look before relying on PUT in production; the example response below is a plausible reconstruction from the source, not a captured live response.

originalRequest.description: Heads up: the controller builds the update as a plain object {name, category, price, stock} with no $set wrapper. MongoDB treats an update object with no operators as a full-document replacement, so fields this endpoint doesn't list - images, description, inStock - are not carried over. This looks like a pre-existing gap from before the `images` field was added (PATCH is unaffected and safe for partial updates). Worth a look before relying on PUT in production; the example response below is a plausible reconstruction from the source, not a captured live response.

response.name: Bad Request - all PUT fields required
Forbidden - customer role cannot delete products

item.name: Cart
item.description: Every cart route requires a Bearer token (any authenticated role) - `router.use(protect)` is applied at the top of cart.routes.js. Non-admin users can only see/modify their own carts; the controller enforces this per-request and returns 403 otherwise. Creating a cart no longer takes `customerName` in the body - it is derived from the logged-in user automatically.

item.name: Orders & Checkout
item.description: Every order route requires a Bearer token. `POST /` (checkout) is the ONLY place stock is reduced and a cart is cleared - there is no separate cart-checkout endpoint. `PATCH /:id/status` is admin-only; everything else is available to the cart's owner (or any admin). If `shippingAddress` is omitted from the checkout body, it falls back to the logged-in user's registered `address` - it is not a hard requirement the way `cartId` is.

request.description: cartId2 was emptied by the Clear Cart request in the Cart folder but is still `active`, so this hits the empty-cart check rather than the status check.

response.name: Bad Request - cart has no items

originalRequest.description: cartId2 was emptied by the Clear Cart request in the Cart folder but is still `active`, so this hits the empty-cart check rather than the status check.
